headline: The White House and Anthropic standoff over pulled cybersecurity models dominated the window as a cluster of safety and policy items moved together.
body: The conflict between the U.S. government and Anthropic escalated, with reporting that the administration forced Anthropic to pull its cybersecurity models and arguments that the Fable jailbreak review was the pretext rather than the cause, since reviewers characterized the model behavior as working as intended. Anthropic also faces a separate lawsuit over alleged false advertising on Claude Max subscription limits. On infrastructure, Microsoft turned to AWS to relieve an AI capacity crunch at GitHub, and AWS WAF added a mechanism letting content owners charge AI bots for access. Breakouts skewed technical, with privacy-preserving inference, conformal prediction, model pruning, and vulnerability detection entering alongside language model and advantage weighting.

cited_items.title: Microsoft turns to AWS as GitHub faces AI capacity crunch
Anthropic Sued over Alleged False Advertising on Claude Max Subscription Limits
The White House Is Ratcheting Up Its War Against Anthropic
AWS WAF now lets content owners charge AI bots for access
The US government’s Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak
